body:
To review props `true` by default that would be a better DX if we change the name to make them `false` by default or use a union type, like:
- `noValidate={false}` → `nativeValidation`, `enableNativeValidation`
- `openMultiple={false}`→ `type:  "single"  |  "multiple"`
  - To consider how this could work for multi select and multi toggles, and whether we want a single default for all of them 
- `alignOptionToTrigger={false}` → `dropdown` ?
  - Side note: still feels odd it's not on `Positioner`

etc.

**Search keywords**:

comments.body:
@mnajdova reviewed the props that are `true`-by-default and changed applicable ones to `false` by default unless the prop name was worse, or changing the value was the less recommended option where the extra friction isn't necessarily bad

--- 

Specifically:

> noValidate={false} → nativeValidation, enableNativeValidation

We recommend Base UI's validation, otherwise you can just use `<form>` anyway

> alignOptionToTrigger={false} → dropdown ?

The names for the reverse are strange, _or_ there was `disableAlignItemWithTrigger` but it's pretty long

labels.name: breaking change
labels.description: Introduces changes that are not backward compatible.

labels.name: scope: all components
labels.description: Widespread work has an impact on almost all components.

title: [all components] Review props that are `true` by default
